articles.title: NEW YORK JETS REINTRODUCES JET TO TEAM LOGO IN NOSTALGIC REBRAND
articles.summary: The New York Jets have reintroduced a jet motif into its logo, which has been updated to look more modern and forward-thinking. Along with the new logo, the team has also launched a Legacy Collection of uniforms that will be worn for the next five seasons, honoring the past while appealing to new fans.

articles.title: ADOBE RELEASES ACROBAT AI ASSISTANT STARTING AT $4.99 A MONTH
articles.summary: Adobe released an AI assistant that helps users understand digital documents. It is also launching a free mobile version of the tool that responds to voice commands and adding extensions to Microsoft Edge and Google Chrome. The assistant can help users locate specific information in PDF files and other documents, generate summaries, and find citations to support their questions using a chatbot interface. Subscriptions start at $4.99/month.

articles.title: BMW I5 FLOW NOSTOKANA - COLOR CHANGING TECHNOLOGY
ALASKA AIRLINES REACHES NEW HEIGHTS WITH THE HELP OF VARIABLES
articles.summary: Figma variables enabled Alaska Airlines to accommodate various screen sizes, light and dark modes, and themes for internal teams and external partners. By articulating primitive, semantic, and component-specific design tokens, the company can now modify an entire design through modes.

articles.title: DESIGNING THE NEW ERA OF TEAMS
articles.summary: The new Microsoft Teams app was crafted from the ground up to be faster, simpler, and more flexible. This post shows the design journey behind making the app more expressive, balanced, and beautiful using Microsoft's Fluent two design language.

articles.title: UNLOCKING ICON USABILITY: INSIGHTS FROM COGNITIVE PSYCHOLOGY
articles.summary: Icons in digital interfaces are guided by cognitive psychology theories influenced by traits like concreteness and visual complexity. Research highlights the importance of optimizing these traits for effective interface design, emphasizing regular user testing and accessibility considerations.

articles.title: PACKAGING DESIGN WITH AI
articles.summary: Tell Packify what you imagine for your packaging design and it will quickly create creative editable concepts.

articles.title: DEBLANK COLORS
articles.summary: Describe your project and discover and visualize color palettes with the help of AI.

articles.title: DESIGN PROPOSAL
articles.summary: Approach your next client with a clean, modern, fully customizable design proposal.

articles.title: WORKING ON THE BCWA NEW IDENTITY
articles.summary: The BC Weightlifting Association sought a fresh brand to promote weightlifting in British Columbia, leading Four Nine Digital to conduct surveys and develop a brand core document. Through sketching, feedback, and iterations, they crafted a logo embodying weightlifting movements and elements of the British Columbia crest.

articles.title: HOW TO REFRAME YOUR FRUSTRATIONS
articles.summary: Wise designers have open minds, question their assumptions, and are rooted in the real world. They can reframe important problems and make them actionable. They don't dwell on what they can't control but take action, reframe their frustrations, and find solutions.

articles.title: UX DESIGN IS RAPIDLY CHANGING — CAN YOU KEEP UP?
articles.summary: UX designers now prioritize product-focused actions and require additional skills like CX process, and parallel skills such as content creation and motion design are increasingly valuable. Despite a saturated job market, there's a shortage of top-tier creatives, prompting the need to justify designers' roles in the face of AI challenges.

articles.title: LONGBOARDING
articles.summary: Defining core use for your product will set the desired end-state for user onboarding.

articles.title: HOW MUCH MEMORY DO YOU NEED TODAY?
articles.summary: This site tells a beautiful visual story of how an '80s timeless classic — the original Macintosh - was restored.

articles.title: THE HORRIFIC UI/UX DESIGN OF HUMANE'S AI PIN
articles.summary: Humane's AI Pin doesn't work consistently, sometimes misidentifying basic facts or not giving answers, and there are many UI and UX issues, starting with the impractical way to unlock it.
